draft.introduction: Diotima’s speech in the Symposium (201d–212a) describes the ascent from love of a single beautiful body to the vision of Beauty itself. This “ladder of love” became the paradigmatic account of spiritual ascent in the Western tradition.

translation.text: Beginning from beautiful things, one must always go upward for the sake of that Beauty, using them as steps: from one body to two, from two to all beautiful bodies, then from beautiful bodies to beautiful practices, from practices to beautiful sciences, until from sciences one arrives at that science which is nothing other than the science of Beauty itself.
translation.original_text: ἀπὸ τῶν καλῶν ἐκείνων ἀεὶ ἐπανιέναι ἐκείνου ἕνεκα τοῦ καλοῦ

translator_notes.note: Diotima’s ascent became the model for Plotinus’s account of the soul’s return to the One and for the Christian mystical tradition’s via negativa. The movement from particulars to the universal Form of Beauty is the heart of Platonic spirituality.
